Create an artisan owned & managed Moroccan craft economy.

The Anou Cooperative is an award winning collective of over 600 artisans from cooperatives, associations, and workshops across Morocco. The Anou Cooperative is owned and managed by Morocco's artisan community and is open to all authentic artisans committed to quality and transparency.

Fair Trade has existed for decades but Moroccan artisans are fewer and poorer than ever. This is because real change in the artisan sector doesn't happen by paying artisans a little extra money. For craft in Morocco to flourish, artisans need wealth, real skills, and most importantly, real power.
Products beautifully displayed in Marrakech or Instagram only net artisans less than 4% of what you pay. Oftentimes, this doesn't even cover the cost of the material of of the product.

Fair trade pays a little better, but not enough to create meaningful sector wide change. The 80% not only represents 80% in lost income, but the real skills in running businesses artisans are never an opportunity to learn.

Through Anou, artisans earn what fair-trade organizations traditionally make. Most important, the 20% is all recycled back into the artisan community through training or employment and creating collective power to change the future of craft.

Mustapha is both a metalsmith of Association Nahda and an Anou artisan leader specializing in operations. Right, Mustapha facilitates a meeting with Cooperative Tadighoust to help them improve their internal bylaws.

The Anou Cooperative selects the most motivated, brightest artisans across Morocco to help run the operations of the cooperative. This ensures that artisans have access to gaining skills mostly reserved for middlemen and foreigners. Learn more.
President & Founder of Association Afous Gafous

As an artisan leaders Fatima Naciri supports groups like Cooperative Timzounine on colors and development of a new rugs. Fatima is an weaver of Association Afous G Afous and specialist in Anou dye work.

Anou uses technology to reduce the complexity of global commerce so that artisans can easily connect with an individual customer in upstate New York, or a purchasing manager from West Elm. Anou is automating traditional middlemen work and ensuring the resulting wealth stays in artisan hands.

For every artisan that joins the community, Anou creates a customized tool that enables to post product to Anou and create comprehensive product pages -- all with clicks on a language free interface demonstrated on the right.
Kenza is a weaver and the president of Association Tithrite. Kenza has flown all around the world to speak about Anou and the change the artisan community is seeking to create.
©2023 The Anou Cooperative | Owned and Managed by Morocco's Artisan Community

The Anou Cooperative is an award winning collective of over 600 artisans from cooperatives, associations,
  and workshops across Morocco. The Anou Cooperative is owned and managed by Morocco's artisan community and 
  is open to all authentic artisans committed to quality and transparency.

Fair Trade has existed for decades but Moroccan artisans are fewer and poorer than ever. 
    This is because real change in the artisan sector doesn't happen by paying artisans a little extra money. 
    For craft in Morocco to flourish, artisans need wealth, real skills, and most importantly, real power.

Products beautifully displayed in Marrakech or Instagram only net artisans less than 4% of what you pay. Oftentimes, 
        this doesn't even cover the cost of the material of of the product.

Fair trade pays a little better, but not enough to create meaningful sector wide change. The 80% not only represents
        80% in lost income, but the real skills in running businesses artisans are never an opportunity to learn.

Through Anou, artisans earn what fair-trade organizations traditionally make. Most important, the 20% is all 
      recycled back into the artisan community through training or employment and creating collective power to change the future

President & Founder of Association Afous Gafous

As an artisan leaders Fatima Naciri supports groups like Cooperative
      Timzounine on colors and development of a new rugs.  Fatima is an weaver of Association Afous G Afous and specialist in Anou dye work.

Anou uses technology to reduce the complexity of global commerce so that artisans can easily connect with an individual
    customer in upstate New York, or a purchasing manager from West Elm. Anou is automating traditional middlemen work and ensuring the resulting 
    wealth stays in artisan hands.

All artisans who make the product they sell are welcome to join Anou provided they agree to Anou's transparency requirements
    and are motivated. When an artisan expresses an interest to join, an Anou artisan leader visits the artisans workshop and 
  verifies that the artisan makes what they sell. After the initial screening, the artisan undergoes a two day training
which includes setting up their profile, an introductory training into pricing, photography and other basics of selling online.

For every artisan that joins the community, Anou creates a customized tool that enables to post product to Anou and create comprehensive product pages 
  -- all with clicks on a language free interface demonstrated on the right.

All orders ship directly from the artisan that made the product. The means your shipment can come from the coast of the atlantic, snow capped peaks of the Atlas, or the deserts of the Anti-Atlas. 
      Despite the distance and geographic challenges, shipments are insured so you can simply just enjoy the journey.

Artisans ship your orders to Anou's artisan run store, where artisan leaders inspect every shipment to make sure it meets our standards. Artisan leaders
    use a proprietary app that enables them to record the results of Anou's six point quality control process and inform the customer.

Kenza is a weaver and the president of Association Tithrite . Kenza has flown all around the world to speak about Anou and the change
        the artisan community is seeking to create.

OUR PROCESS Anou uses technology to reduce the complexity of global commerce so that artisans can easily connect with an individual
    customer in upstate New York, or a purchasing manager from West Elm. Anou is automating traditional middlemen work and ensuring the resulting 
    wealth stays in artisan hands.
Training & Onboarding Adding Products Shipping Quality

All artisans who make the product they sell are welcome to join Anou provided they agree to Anou's transparency requirements
    and are motivated. When an artisan expresses an interest to join, an Anou artisan leader visits the artisans workshop and 
  verifies that the artisan makes what they sell. After the initial screening, the artisan undergoes a two day training
which includes setting up their profile, an introductory training into pricing, photography and other basics of selling online.
